tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":56935,"title":"Add automated version bump pipeline for Azure Management Generator (AzureManagementGeneratorVersion)","body":"## Context

The Azure Generator (http-client-csharp) has an automated pipeline that bumps its version in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props` whenever a new build is published. This happens through the `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template's Regenerate stage, which:

1. Publishes the new generator package to the Azure DevOps NuGet feed
2. Updates the emitter package JSON with the new version
3. Regenerates SDK clients
4. Creates a PR with title "Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}"

The management generator (http-client-csharp-mgmt) should have the same automated version bump behavior for `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`.

## Current State

- `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion` and `AzureGeneratorVersion` are automatically bumped by CI pipelines
- `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently added and is currently updated manually
- The mgmt generator CI (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`) already uses the same `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template, but the version bump for the NuGet package reference in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` may not be wired up

## Expected Behavior

When a new version of the mgmt generator is published from the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` CI pipeline, `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` should be automatically updated, mirroring how the Azure Generator pipeline updates `AzureGeneratorVersion`.

## Reference

- PR comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700
- Azure Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml`
- Mgmt Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`
- Shared template: `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`
- Version props file: `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props`","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-11T05:18:32Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:18:32Z"}
{"color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is owned by the Engineering System team.","id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMzU0MDc0MzU1","name":"Central-EngSys"}

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4055899539,"number":56935,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add automated version bump pipeline for Azure Management Generator (AzureManagementGeneratorVersion)","body":"## Context

The Azure Generator (http-client-csharp) has an automated pipeline that bumps its version in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props` whenever a new build is published. This happens through the `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template's Regenerate stage, which:

1. Publishes the new generator package to the Azure DevOps NuGet feed
2. Updates the emitter package JSON with the new version
3. Regenerates SDK clients
4. Creates a PR with title "Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}"

The management generator (http-client-csharp-mgmt) should have the same automated version bump behavior for `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`.

## Current State

- `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion` and `AzureGeneratorVersion` are automatically bumped by CI pipelines
- `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently added and is currently updated manually
- The mgmt generator CI (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`) already uses the same `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template, but the version bump for the NuGet package reference in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` may not be wired up

## Expected Behavior

When a new version of the mgmt generator is published from the `http-client-csharp-mgmt` CI pipeline, `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` should be automatically updated, mirroring how the Azure Generator pipeline updates `AzureGeneratorVersion`.

## Reference

- PR comment: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700
- Azure Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml`
- Mgmt Generator CI: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml`
- Shared template: `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml`
- Version props file: `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props`","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-11T05:18:32Z","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:18:32Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56935/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7xwB2T"}]}

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** Feature request to add an automated version bump pipeline for the Azure Management Generator (`AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props`), mirroring the existing automation for `AzureGeneratorVersion` and `UnbrandedGeneratorVersion`.

Filed by a **repo member** (@ArcturusZhang). Not customer-reported.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

**Type:** Feature request / engineering system improvement  
**Area:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` — Azure Management Generator CI pipeline  
**Priority indicators:** Medium — currently requires manual intervention each time a new mgmt generator build is published, creating toil and potential for version drift

**Root cause / gap identified:**  
`AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` was recently introduced but the `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` template's Regenerate stage does not yet wire up the automated NuGet package version bump to `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` for the mgmt generator. The other two generators (`UnbrandedGenerator`, `AzureGenerator`) already have this working.

**Key files involved:**
| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` | Mgmt generator CI — already uses `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` |
| `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml` | Azure generator CI — reference working implementation |
| `eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` | Shared template with the Regenerate stage |
| `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Generation.Packages.props` | Where `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion` lives |

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested Tasks / Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Compare `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml` vs `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` to identify what parameters are passed to `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` for the Regenerate stage
- [ ] Check if `archetype-typespec-emitter.yml` already accepts a parameter to control which property in `Directory.Generation.Packages.props` gets bumped (e.g., `generatorVersionPropertyName`)
- [ ] If the template already supports it, add/fix the parameter in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml` pointing to `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`
- [ ] If the template does NOT support a configurable property name yet, extend the template to accept a NuGet package version property name parameter
- [ ] Verify the PR title format `"Update {emitterName} version to prerelease {emitterVersion}"` is produced correctly for the mgmt generator case
- [ ] Test end-to-end by triggering a prerelease build of the mgmt generator and confirming the auto-bump PR is created

</details>

<details>
<summary>💡 Investigation Starting Points</summary>

1. **Diff the two CI files** to see what's different:
   ```
   diff eng/packages/http-client-csharp/ci.yml eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/ci.yml
   ```

2. **Check the shared template** for any parameter that controls the `props` property name:
   ```
   grep -n "GeneratorVersion\|generatorVersion\|packageVersionPropertyName" eng/common/pipelines/templates/archetype-typespec-emitter.yml
   ```

3. **Reference PR** that introduced `AzureManagementGeneratorVersion`: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627#discussion_r2915155700

</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Ownership</summary>

Per CODEOWNERS, the owners of `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` are:
@JoshLove-msft, @m-nash, @jorgerangel-msft, @live1206, @ArcturusZhang

</details>
